mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling in equities and negative forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in greater than 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 5.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in greater than 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that compress profit margins, leading to negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles yield positive 20-day returns in greater than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising interest rates combined with falling unemployment creates stagflationary pressure that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, depressing equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in greater than 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 1.0% annualized signals economic contraction, prompting immediate portfolio de-risking and negative short-term equity returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth versus 60-day returns remains below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation exceeding 3.0% while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0% creates negative real rates that erode purchasing power and force eventual policy tightening, generating negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The negative real rate regime yields positive 20-day returns in greater than 55% of observed months.
